name: Cornmeal Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons fat (melted butter or margarine, olive or vegetable oil, bacon drippings or chicken fat)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small mixing bowl, sift together the cornmeal, flour, baking powder, and salt.
2. In a separate bowl, beat the egg yolk with milk and fat.
3. Stir the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients until just combined.
4. Drop spoonfuls of the dough onto simmering vegetables, stew, soup, or stock.
5. Cover tightly and cook over low heat for 15 minutes.
6. Serve hot. Makes 2 to 3 dumplings, enough for 1 generous serving (can be doubled).
Note: When doubling the recipe, use 1 whole egg instead of 2 egg yolks.
